data.body:
BRAINSTORM decision log (2/N).

Q2 — Contract HOME: FRONT-MATTER LIGHT + SCHEMA-REGISTRY NAMES. A prompt's contract lives in its OWN .md YAML front-matter (input_schema: <name>, output_schema: <name>, execution_mode, category, and reviewer-only dimension/applies_to), but heavy JSON Schemas stay referenced BY NAME in src/rebar/schemas/ (reusing today's output_schema name-indirection). reviewers/catalog.json COLLAPSES into per-file front-matter; the catalog becomes a DERIVED index (scan prompt files to build it), not the source of truth. Editor edits names + text only => item-4 prompt-text write-back stays low-corruption-risk (no giant inline schemas to round-trip).

Q3 — Editor VALIDATION model: TYPE-AWARE EXPRESSION VALIDATION + READ-ONLY INSPECTOR (NOT visualized dataflow ports). Connections stay BPMN control-flow. Extend lint_refs.py: when a step's with: maps a field to ${{ steps.A.outputs.foo }}, check (a) A's output_contract actually produces 'foo' and (b) foo's type is compatible with this step's input_contract for that field; red error at save/lint. Inspector/properties panel shows each step's consumes/produces (read-only) + autocomplete of available upstream outputs when authoring with:. No drawn typed ports (that paradigm is round-trip-fragile — the class of bug branch UX already hit — and a much larger build). Possible future story only if expression-authoring proves too hard.

Q4 — mcp_call step SCOPE: EXTERNAL MCP SERVERS (the novel power), not rebar's own tools. mcp_call targets external servers configured for the workflow (serena/github/Atlassian/etc.) — the capability scripted ops can't provide (Dify/Rivet 'deterministic MCP step' pattern). rebar's own ticket ops stay as scripted ops (no duplication). Brings the hard parts: server-connection config, tool discovery to surface inputSchema in the palette, opaque-output fallback (any) when MCP outputSchema is absent, and an allowlist/sandbox for safety (cf. Flowise RCE CVE-2025-59528). Runtime: rebar runner is Python; pydantic_ai (already the runner) ships MCP-client support to reuse.
